title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Will term insurance cover me for health-related expenses too?
Meta description: A term insurance plan provides death benefits. But can it also cover health-related expenses? Continue reading to find out.
Keywords: term health insurance, term insurance with health insurance, term insurance premium calculator, term health insurance plans
A term insurance plan is designed to offer your family a financial safety net in the event of an unfortunate incident. While it may provide a death benefit to the policyholder’s nomineesupon the said individual’s demise, will it cover health-related expenses as well? If this question is on your mind, then here’s something that can help clear it out. But before we go ahead, let’s quickly run through the concept of term insurance.
What is Term Insurance?
Term insurance is a type of life insurance plan. In a term insurance plan, the insurer agrees to provide a life cover amount to an individual, known as the policyholder.
This life cover is basically a predetermined sum of money and is issued for a specific period of time, known as the policy term. Now, in case of policyholder’s demise within the policy term , the insurance provider will be obligated under the terms and conditions of the insurance plan to pay the life cover amount to the deceased policyholder’s nominees.
The affected family may make use of this payout to sustain their lifestyle and fulfil their life goals as they see fit. However, in exchange for this life cover, the policyholder has to make regular periodic payments to the insurer, known as premiums. As long as the premiums are paid, the life cover continues to be in place.
But, if the policyholder survives till the end of the policy term, no monetary or maturity benefits would be payable except in case of term insurance plans with return of premium option. This is precisely why a term insurance plan is also referred to as a pure insurance product. Since a term plan without option of return of premium as maturity benefit, only provides death benefits and no maturity benefits, the premium that you would have to pay towards such a policy would also be affordable. Finding it hard to believe? Use a term insurance premium calculator and see for yourself.
What kind of Health Related Expense May Occur?
Before we go ahead and take a look at whether a term plan can cover health related expenses, let’s take a look at the kind of health-related expenses that you may encounter in your day-to-day life.
Expenses for preventive health checkups
Costs that you incur towards tests that help detect illnesses and diseases at an early stage is one of the major health-related expenses that you may incur.
Expenses for treating illnesses, ailments, and diseases
If you’re diagnosed with any illness, ailment, or a disease, the costs that you incur for treating the same is an important health-related expense that may occur. These expenses usually tend to be quite high and can put a strain on your finances; especially when it involves complex procedures or surgeries.
Transport and Hospitalisation expenses
Although comparatively minor, the costs for transporting you to the hospital and the expenses associated with being an in-patient undergoing treatment at a hospital are also few of the popular health-related expenses that you may have to bear.
Medicine expenses
The costs associated with the purchase of medicines and medicinal equipment also forms a major part of health-related expenses that many individuals tend to face as part of their life.
Will My Term Insurance Cover it All?
While there are no ‘term health insurance plans’ per se, you can still ensure that a term plan provides coverage for health-related expenses as well. How, you ask? Here’s a brief explanation.
At the time of purchase of a term insurance plan, you can choose to opt for riders. Riders are essentially add-ons that you can choose to include for a nominal additional premium. These add-ons basically enhance the coverage under specific circumstances by providing you with benefits over and above the base term plan. One such rider, the critical illness rider, can help you cover the costs associated withcritical illnesses listed in the said plan.
The critical illness rider covers certain specified illnesses and diseases. And just like a term insurance plan, the rider also pays out a predetermined sum of money, also known as rider sum assured, to the policyholder. This sum assured is a lump sum payment that’s over and above anything offered by the base term insurance plan.
Now, when a policyholder with the critical illness rider develops an illness or disease that’s specified in the rider terms and conditions, the insurer will pay the rider sum assured. The policyholder can make use of this lump sum rider sum assured payment to cover all the health-related expenses that he might have to incur in connection with treating the illness or the disease.
Conclusion
Although the payout from the critical illness rider can be used to cover health-related expense, it is only disbursed to the policyholder upon diagnosis of the illnesses or diseases specified in the rider terms and conditions. If the policyholder develops illnesses or diseases that are not covered by the rider, they would not be eligible for a payout under the rider.
That said, although there are no specific term health insurance policies that can cover health-related expenses for all known illnesses and diseases, there’s still a way for policyholders. How, you ask? Through the purchase of term insurance withcritical illness rider chosen as an option.
By possessing term insurance withrider option chosen along with it, policyholders can give themselves comprehensive protection from health-related expenses that they may have to incur. However, here’s something that you should note. Since this would require purchasing two different insurance plans, the total premium that you would have to pay towards the both of them may turn out to be quite significant.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
